mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate levels have an inverse relationship with forward equity returns: a lower unemployment rate signals robust labor market conditions that drive corporate earnings and consumer spending, supporting higher S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between UNRATE (negative) and 5-day forward returns is non-negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 2% indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Whenever FEDFUNDS > 2%, the average 60-day forward excess return is non-negative, or the signal fails to produce a negative Rank IC over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and retail sales signals synchronized expansion in both supply and demand, reinforcing corporate earnings and driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and RSAFS are above their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Rank IC of the combined interaction signal is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the daily spread between scenarios (both above vs not) is not positive as predicted.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle housing boom where housing starts are above 1500 (cyclically high), further increases in starts signal overbuilding and impending correction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is above 1500, the average 20-day return is non-negative, or the signal direction does not reverse in the assumed regime relative to non-regime periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll gains exceeding 200,000 indicate strong job creation that boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months with payroll gains above 200k, the average 5-day forward return is not positive, or the signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is high (CPI YoY > 3%) and the federal funds rate is below the inflation rate, real interest rates are negative, eroding purchasing power and leading to negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 3% and FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY
failure_condition: When the trigger condition holds, the average 60-day return is non-negative, or the interaction signal Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a full employment regime (UNRATE below 4%), any further decline in unemployment tightens labor markets excessively, sparking wage inflation and preemptive Fed tightening that depresses equity values over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4% and declining, the average 20-day forward return is positive, or the signal produces positive returns more often than negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales indicate robust consumer demand that directly boosts corporate revenues and profits, supporting higher S&P 500 values over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of RSAFS levels against 60-day forward returns is non-positive (i.e., higher RSAFS does not predict higher returns) over any 2-year window.
